displayTitle: Inside the Probe: One Sandwich, Many Jobs

beats.narration: Open a probe catalog — hundreds of models, and every one a variation on the same sandwich. Let's take it apart.
Top to bottom: a tungsten loaded backing absorbs rearward sound and damps ringing, the thin piezoelectric element, then the wear face meeting the part.

payload.heading: One sandwich inside every probe
payload.note: THE BACKING SETS THE PULSE LENGTH

beats.narration: Three workhorses: straight beams fire straight down for thickness, angle beams ride a wedge into welds, and duals cross two beams for corrosion.
Three performance words: sensitivity detects small reflectors, resolution separates echoes arriving close together, and damping is the knob that trades them.
So the rule: heavy damping means a short pulse — sharp echoes, less punch. Light damping reverses it. Buy the probe to fit the job.

payload.sub: Heavy damping trades sensitivity for resolution.
